item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you very much! I was pretty fun to work on and I'm glad you liked it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for the upvote and the nice comment! :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Awesome. An SCP I simply enjoyed to read.

+1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Much thanks for updoot, am glad you like the wholesomeness

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks! It's certainly gone through a fair bit of changes in the concept phase to get where it is now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is wholesome as fuck and I love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Aww, that's so sweet but sad at the same time.

Yet another thing about the article that toys with my emotions. Well done to you both, Davey and Zyn!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the nice comment!

Is the mouse Diana? Does the mouse just carry on the story of Diana? Or is it something else entirely?

Spoilers!
hide

The mouse is not Diana. The mouse just misses his Diana so much that he came back from the dead to see her again. And when he couldn't find her, he tried to find a new Diana. He made do with whatever he had.

The closer the mouse's caretaker is to the original Diana, the more the mouse identifies with them and wants to stay with them, and the less the mouse's effect negatively affects the primary caretaker.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fanceh mous!!

Mice are actually very smart. Lookit these smart mice:

  • doing tricks https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=t8XZ-_EgWB8
  • buying treats https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=WYjTWJYsbdI
  • buying a smol toy doggo https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=cnN-JN0bYbI

Thanks for the updoot

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I enjoyed this article. I'll be honest, the death of a family member (even if fictional) does pull at my heartstrings a bit, so I did feel bad for Mr. [REDACTED] by the end. I do have a question, though.

Is the mouse Diana? Does the mouse just carry on the story of Diana? Or is it something else entirely?

No matter what the answer to that is, you have my upvote for this wonderful article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fancy Mouse indeed. +1

There's something here that really pulls at my heart. Maybe it's that the mouse is a sort of legacy for Diana, but it's really similar to personal things I've experienced recently, and this hit me harder than I anticipated.

item.title: Re: Author's Post
item.content:encoded:
Big thanks to everyone who reviewed this in chat and was patient about this getting posted today! This has been a heck of a process, since different timezones can make for some hits and misses in being online at the same time.

I really enjoyed working on this! It's been fun writing about this little mouse who just wants to share the gift he's discovered with his beloved owner.

item.title: Author's Post
item.content:encoded:
A collab by DaveyTheDuck (Me) and Zyn.

In loving memory of Gloria Brennan, My grandmother who died a few months after this was made, I miss you.

Photo used: https://www.flickr.com/photos/engyles/19183269033/ (CC BY 2.0)

1880-1930 Slang was researched from https://www.alphadictionary.com/slang/?term=&beginEra=1880&endEra=1930&clean=true&submitsend=Search

Reviewers: Galaxy-Class, taylor_itkin, Taffeta, Ensophos, and Apoplexic. Thank you, dudes.
